title: galium andrewsii subsp. gatense
intro: Galium andrewsii subsp. gatense, also known as Oregon ginger or yellow bird's beak, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of essential oils and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Galium andrewsii subsp. gatense include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin irritations.
